verified.notes: Income limits are based on 200% of federal poverty level (2025 guidelines: $31,300 annual for household of 1, $42,300 for household of 2). Monthly equivalents calculated by dividing annual by 12. Phone number is for United Way of the Midlands Weatherization program in Douglas County; other service providers exist statewide. No age or asset limits found in search results. Program remains active as of April 2026.

programs.programName: Nebraska Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)

verified.notes: Age requirement (55+) confirmed from Nebraska Department of Labor official source. Income limit exists (125% of federal poverty level per multiple sources) but specific dollar amounts not provided in search results. No asset limits found in search results. Official phone number for Nebraska SCSEP not found in provided search results; users directed to contact local Nebraska Department of Labor reemployment services sites per official source.

verified.notes: The Nebraska Community Aging Services Act (Neb. Rev. Stat. § 81-2201 et seq.) defines community aging services and establishes the framework for service delivery, but the search results do not contain specific eligibility criteria (age, income, or asset limits) or contact phone numbers. The statute defines services as those fulfilling the Act's goals to promote self-sufficiency for older persons, but does not specify a minimum age in the provided excerpt. The Nebraska Department of Health and Human Services, Division of Medicaid and Long-Term Care, State Unit on Aging administers the plan. Additional official DHHS documentation would be needed to retrieve current eligibility requirements and contact information.

programs.notes: The Nebraska Community Aging Services Act (Neb. Rev. Stat. § 81-2201 et seq.) defines community aging services and establishes the framework for service delivery, but the search results do not contain specific eligibility criteria (age, income, or asset limits) or contact phone numbers. The statute defines services as those fulfilling the Act's goals to promote self-sufficiency for older persons, but does not specify a minimum age in the provided excerpt. The Nebraska Department of Health and Human Services, Division of Medicaid and Long-Term Care, State Unit on Aging administers the plan. verified.notes: The Aging and Disability Resource Center (ADRC) is a state agency providing information and assistance on community services and supports related to disability and aging, not an eligibility-based program with age, income, or asset requirements. The search results describe ADRC as a resource and referral service coordinating access to various programs (OAA-funded services, Medicaid Waiver programs, etc.), rather than a standalone program with its own eligibility criteria. No 'Aging & Disability Resource Center Act' program with specific eligibility thresholds was identified in the provided sources. The ADRC itself does not appear to have minimum age requirements, income limits, or asset tests. A main contact phone number for Nebraska ADRC was not provided in the search results.
